cards.grammarNote: aller uses être as auxiliary. Feminine subject (elle) → past participle allée gets -e agreement. au = à + le.

cards.back: Nous sommes arrivés à la gare
cards.grammarNote: arriver uses être. Masculine (or mixed) plural subject → past participle arrivés gets -s. à la gare = feminine noun, no contraction needed.
partir uses être. Masculine singular subject → participle stays parti (no change). du = de + le.

cards.front: They came out of the museum (mixed group)
cards.grammarNote: sortir uses être. Mixed or masculine plural → sortis (-s). Feminine plural would be sorties (-es).
entrer uses être. Feminine subject → entrée. dans = inside a space (versus à which marks a destination).

cards.front: He went up to the top floor
cards.back: Il est monté au dernier étage
cards.grammarNote: monter uses être when intransitive (no object). Note: if monter takes a direct object ('he carried the suitcase upstairs'), it switches to avoir.

cards.front: She came down to the lobby
cards.back: Elle est descendue dans le hall
cards.grammarNote: descendre uses être when intransitive. Feminine → descendue. Like monter, it switches to avoir when it has a direct object.
rester uses être. Feminine plural → restées (-ées). l'hôtel: elision before h aspiré (h aspiré blocks liaison but not elision — le → l' here).
tomber uses être. Masculine singular → tombé (no change). DR MRS VANDERTRAMP lists tombé under T.
naître uses être. Feminine subject → née. Note double -e in the written form. The masculine is né.

cards.front: He came back to the apartment
cards.grammarNote: rentrer (to return home/back) uses être. Masculine singular → rentré. Distinguish from retourner (to go back to a place other than home).

cards.back: Elle est passée devant le marché
cards.grammarNote: passer uses être when intransitive. Feminine → passée. devant = in front of / past. Like monter/descendre, passer takes avoir when it has a direct object.
